promptPreview:
Извлеки ВСЕ биографические факты из статьи о Carl Rogers.

ФОКУС ЭТОГО ПРОХОДА
Персона: Carl Rogers. Это часть 1 из 2. Извлекай ВСЕ факты из этого фрагмента — включая мелкие семейные детали, конкретны
responseText:
[
  {"year": 1902, "text": "Carl Ransom Rogers was born on January 8 in Oak Park, Illinois", "category": "birth", "sphere": "family", "month": 1},
  {"year": 1922, "text": "Rogers traveled to Beijing, China, for an international Christian conference", "category": "other", "sphere": "place"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Rogers graduated from the University of Wisconsin", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Rogers married fellow Wisconsin student Helen Elliott", "category": "family", "sphere": "family"},
  {"year": 1924, "text": "Rogers enrolled at Union Theological Seminary in New York City", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1927, "text": "Rogers obtained an M.A. from Teachers College, Columbia University", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1927, "text": "Rogers began an internship at the Institute for Child Guidance", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1928, "text": "Rogers completed his internship at the Institute for Child Guidance", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1930, "text": "Rogers served as director of the Society for the Prevention of Cruelty to Children", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1931, "text": "Rogers obtained a Ph.D. from Teachers College, Columbia University", "category": "education", "sphere": "education"},
  {"year": 1935, "text": "Rogers began lecturing at the University of Rochester", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1939, "text": "Rogers published the book The Clinical Treatment of the Problem Child", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1940, "text": "Rogers became professor of clinical psychology at Ohio State University", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1941, "text": "Elias Porter published empirical evidence of the client-centered approach", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1942, "text": "Rogers wrote his second book, Counseling and Psychotherapy", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1945, "text": "Rogers was invited to set up a counseling center at the University of Chicago", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1945, "text": "Rogers became a professor of psychology at the University of Chicago", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1946, "text": "Rogers co-authored Counseling with Returned Servicemen with John L. Wallen", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1947, "text": "Rogers was elected president of the American Psychological Association", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1951, "text": "Rogers published the book Client-Centered Therapy", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1954, "text": "Rogers published the book Psychotherapy and Personality Change", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1956, "text": "Rogers received the Award for Distinguished Scientific Contributions from the APA", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1956, "text": "Rogers became the first president of the American Academy of Psychotherapists", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1957, "text": "Rogers began teaching psychology at the University of Wisconsin, Madison", "category": "career", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1961, "text": "Rogers published the book On Becoming a Person", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1961, "text": "Rogers was elected a Fellow of the American Academy of Arts and Sciences", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1963, "text": "Rogers left the University of Wisconsin to join the Western Behavioral Sciences Institute", "category": "move", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1968, "text": "Rogers helped found the Center for Studies of the Person", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1969, "text": "Rogers published the book Freedom to Learn", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1972, "text": "Rogers received the Award for Distinguished Professional Contributions to Psychology from the APA", "category": "award", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1974, "text": "Rogers began a series of Person-Centered Approach Workshops", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1977, "text": "Rogers published the book Carl Rogers on Personal Power", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1983, "text": "Rogers published the book Freedom to Learn for the '80s", "category": "publication", "sphere": "creativity"},
  {"year": 1984, "text": "Rogers concluded his series of Person-Centered Approach Workshops", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1985, "text": "Rogers facilitated the Rust Peace Workshop in Rust, Austria", "category": "project", "sphere": "career"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Rogers suffered a fall resulting in a fractured pelvis", "category": "health", "sphere": "health"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Rogers underwent a successful operation for his fractured pelvis", "category": "health", "sphere": "health"},
  {"year": 1987, "text": "Rogers died on February 4 following a heart attack", "category": "death", "sphere": "health", "month": 2}
]
